info.name: Microsoft Exchange Microsoft Graph Mail API

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return in a result set

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip in a result set

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

params.name: $filter
params.description: OData filter expression to filter results

params.name: $orderby
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to sort results by

params.name: $search
params.description: Search string to filter results

items.docs: Get all the messages in the signed-in user's mailbox including the Deleted Items and Clutter folders. Supports OData query parameters including $filter, $orderby, $select, $top, $skip, and $expand.
Create a draft of a new message in the signed-in user's mailbox. The draft can then be updated and sent. The message is saved in the Drafts folder by default.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

items.docs: Read properties and relationships of a message object. Supports $select query parameter to return specific properties.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Update the properties of a message object. Only draft messages can have their body, subject, and other writable properties updated.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Delete a message in the specified user's mailbox, or delete a relationship of the message. The message is moved to the Deleted Items folder.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Send a previously created message draft. The message is then saved in the Sent Items folder. The draft message can be a new message draft, reply draft, reply-all draft, or a forward draft.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Reply to the sender of a message, add a comment, and send the reply. The message is then saved in the Sent Items folder.

info.name: Microsoft Exchange Reply all to message

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Reply to all recipients of a message, add a comment, and send the reply. The message is then saved in the Sent Items folder.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Forward a message, add a comment, and send the forwarded message. The message is then saved in the Sent Items folder.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Copy a message to a specified folder. Returns the new copy of the message in the destination folder.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Move a message to a specified folder. This creates a new copy of the message in the destination folder and removes the original message.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Create a draft of a reply message. You can then update the draft and send it.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Create a draft of a reply-all message. You can then update the draft and send it.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Create a draft of a forward message. You can then update the draft to add recipients and content, and then send it.

Send the message specified in the request body using either JSON or MIME format. The message is saved in the Sent Items folder by default. You can include a file attachment in the same sendMail action call.

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

items.docs: Retrieve a list of attachment objects attached to a message. Includes file attachments, item attachments, and reference attachments.

info.name: Microsoft Exchange Add attachment to message

params.name: message-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the message

items.docs: Add a file, item, or reference attachment to a message. This operation limits the size of the attachment to 3 MB. For larger attachments, use the upload session approach.

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return in a result set

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip in a result set

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

params.name: includeHiddenFolders
params.description: Include hidden mail folders in the response

items.docs: Get all the mail folders in the signed-in user's mailbox, including any mail search folders. By default, hidden mail folders are not included. Use the includeHiddenFolders query parameter to include them.
Create a new mail folder in the root folder of the user's mailbox.

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

items.docs: Read the properties and relationships of a mail folder object.

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

items.docs: Update the properties of a mail folder object.

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

items.docs: Delete the specified mail folder. The folder is moved to the Deleted Items folder. To permanently delete a mail folder, use the permanentDelete action.

info.name: Microsoft Exchange List messages in mail folder

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return in a result set

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip in a result set

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

params.name: $filter
params.description: OData filter expression to filter results

params.name: $orderby
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to sort results by

items.docs: Get all the messages in the specified mail folder of the signed-in user's mailbox.

info.name: Microsoft Exchange Create message in folder

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

items.docs: Create a new message in the specified mail folder.

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return in a result set

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip in a result set

items.docs: Get the folder collection under the specified folder.

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

items.docs: Create a new child mail folder under the specified folder.

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

items.docs: Copy a mail folder and its contents to another mail folder.

params.name: mailFolder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name such as inbox, drafts, sentitems, or deleteditems

items.docs: Move a mail folder and its contents to another mail folder.
